title: Mangrove Deforestation Detection And Coastal Protection Loss In Southeast Asia
summary: Build a verified workflow for linking satellite-detected mangrove deforestation to quantified coastal-protection service loss in Southeast Asia, with explicit deforestation-driver documentation, storm-surge attenuation methodology, and remaining-mangrove threat assessment.
why_it_matters: Southeast Asia has lost over 30 percent of its mangroves since 1980, primarily to shrimp aquaculture and coastal development. Mangroves reduce storm-surge height by 30-70 percent and provide nursery habitat for commercially important fisheries. Global Mangrove Watch provides annual 25m-resolution maps, but the analytical chain from deforestation pixels to quantified coastal-protection loss for specific communities has not been built. Without verified protection-loss analysis, coastal-zone management decisions rely on qualitative arguments that cannot compete with the economic incentives driving aquaculture expansion.

success_metrics.target: Every mangrove-loss claim links to GMW or equivalent satellite product with resolution, temporal coverage, accuracy assessment, and driver classification methodology.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against satellite deforestation documentation.

success_metrics.target: Every coastal-protection-loss estimate documents storm-surge model integration, mangrove-width attenuation relationship, and population-at-risk methodology.
success_metrics.validation_method: Replicator confirms protection-loss methodology and validates against historical flood records where available.

success_metrics.target: No mangrove-loss analysis is merged without explicit driver classification (aquaculture, agriculture, development) and documentation of the economic incentives driving conversion.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer confirms driver-classification methodology and economic context for each deforestation hotspot.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, protection-valuation safety review, and replication for quantitative outputs.
